hero.description: I build useful products, especially when backend, data, UI and production need to come together without a huge team around them.
hero.note: I care more about readable systems, restrained interfaces and honest write-ups than polished landing-page promises.

project.title: Current Project
project.subtitle: The thing taking most of my time right now.

rea.name: Real Estate Analytics Platform
rea.problem: Buying a property from an average price per square meter is still too shaky for a real decision.
rea.solution: I combine market data, deterministic rules and an AI layer to produce analysis that stays fast to read but easy to justify.
rea.output: A decision-support report you can use in seconds, with the assumptions still visible.
rea.context: It is the kind of product I like building: full stack, concrete trade-offs, short iterations, and no fake polish hiding the hard parts.

contact.title: Need a product + engineering perspective?
contact.subtitle: I can help on a Spring backend, a Vue/Angular frontend, architecture questions, or simply challenge an idea before too much code gets written.

layout.sidebarNote: I mostly share field notes, in-progress products and explicit technical choices here — not a polished showcase pretending everything is finished.
layout.footerNote: The site stays intentionally simple: readable, fast, and updated alongside real work.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: I prefer clear systems, explicit trade-offs and projects that are genuinely useful to someone.

intro.title: Who am I?
intro.content: Passionate developer with expertise in full-stack web development. I enjoy building performant and maintainable applications, following industry best practices.
paragraphs: I mostly work on products where business logic, code and production constraints all need to fit together without turning into a mess.
Over the last few years I have worked across Spring, Vue, Angular, Kafka, OAuth2, Keycloak, Kubernetes and plenty of less glamorous but very real integrations.
I like writing about incidents, architecture decisions and design-system drift because that is usually where the useful lessons are.

footer.builtWith: Built by hand with Nuxt and Tailwind
